% --- Chapter: The Lighthouse ---
\chapter{The Lighthouse}

It was the kind of morning that arrives only at the edge of the world, with \emph{fog} and a \textbf{pewter} sea, tended by the keeper of the \textsc{Eddystone} light\footnote{A note anchored inline, as footnotes are.}.

\section{A Letter Arrives}

Even prose with awkward characters typesets cleanly: 50\% off, A \& B, \#1, and a\_b.

\begin{quotation}
What a person does with news that arrives too late to be useful and too early to be ignored.
\end{quotation}

\begin{center}* * *\end{center}

\begin{verse}
The fog made up its mind at last \\
and, very slowly, began to lift.
\end{verse}

\begin{quote}\itshape
She had counted those steps for eleven years.
\end{quote}
